_comment: Example AIP Approval Envelope — AIP spec §4.6.3–§4.6.5. Submitted as the request body to POST /v1/approvals. This example models a two-step financial workflow: (1) place an e-commerce order, (2) debit a bank account (triggered after step 1 completes). Step 2 has a compensation step (refund) in case execution fails after the order is placed. Signatures and hashes are placeholders.
description: Place an e-commerce order for office supplies (up to £150) and process the associated bank payment. If the payment fails after the order is placed, the order will be automatically cancelled.

steps.description: Place order #ORD-2026-04-001 for office supplies totalling £150.00 at shop.example.com
Debit £150.00 from account ending 4242 to settle order #ORD-2026-04-001

compensation_steps.description: Cancel order #ORD-2026-04-001 if the bank payment fails after the order has been placed

_decoded_creator_signature_input._note: creator_signature is EdDSA over JCS(envelope with creator_signature set to ''). The canonical field order for signing: approval_id, created_by, principal_id, description, approval_window_expires_at, created_at, steps, compensation_steps, total_value, currency, creator_signature.

_notes.status_on_submission: The Registry sets status to 'pending_approval' on receipt. The status field is READ-ONLY and MUST NOT be set by the submitter.
_notes.total_value_computation: total_value = sum of ALL step value fields (required AND optional) = 150 (step 1) + 150 (step 2) = 300. Both required:true steps carry value. This represents the maximum financial exposure.
_notes.triggered_by: Step 2 triggered_by:1 means it can only be claimed after step 1 has status 'completed'. Step 1 has triggered_by:null (root step — claimable immediately on approval).
_notes.compensation: compensation_steps[0] is compensation for step 2 (compensation_index:0). If step 2 fails after step 1 completes, the orchestrator will execute the cancel compensation without new human approval.
_notes.notification: When notification_uri is present, the Registry POSTs to that URI on pending_approval transition with {approval_id, description, principal_id, review_uri}.
_notes.principal_signature: Added by the Registry after the principal approves via their wallet. Covers JCS(full envelope with principal_signature set to '').
_notes.step_execution_tokens: After approval, the orchestrating agent requests short-lived Step Execution Tokens from POST /v1/approvals/{approval_id}/steps/{step_index}/claim for each step at execution time.
